race.name: BP MS 150
race.tagline: 5,000+ riders crush 150 miles over two days for MS, Texas-style grit and glory.

vitals.location: Houston to College Station, Texas, USA
vitals.field_size: Over 5,000 riders swarm from Houston and Austin
vitals.registration: Tiered fees $100-$140 plus $400 fundraising minimum; includes T-shirt at $500, medal, jersey perks
vitals.feed_zones: Every 8-18 miles with hydration, snacks, mechanics, medical, SAG wagons
vitals.cutoff_time: Self-paced with full support, no hard cutoffs
route_options: Houston Full: 100mi Day 1 + 80mi Day 2 — epic grind
Austin Full: 65-100mi Day 1 + 45-80mi Day 2 — scenic twist
Short Options: 38mi Bastrop route, Leap Ahead shortcuts

climate.description: Late April brings balmy 70-85°F temps with rising humidity that saps strength by midday. Expect possible light rain turning roads slick amid Gulf breezes. Nights cool to 60°F for recovery in La Grange tents.

terrain.surface: Smooth paved county roads wind through ranchlands and state parks. Fresh asphalt dominates with occasional chip-seal grit. Tree canopies shade Bastrop stretches amid open prairie exposure.

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 76. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 74.

final_verdict.one_liner: 5,000+ riders crush 150 miles over two days for MS, Texas-style grit and glory.

race_day_tips.text: Slow down and be careful during certain sections of the race.

additional_quotes.text: I can't believe they're letting us on the field - that is a hundred times better than Austin

rider_intel.search_text: The BP MS 150 from Houston to College Station receives positive feedback from riders, with one participant noting the race experience is significantly better than Austin events. The transcript suggests there are sections where riders need to exercise caution and slow down. Participants express surprise and appreciation for being allowed access to certain areas during the race, though specific course details and terrain conditions are not detailed in the available video content.
